files_to_touch.change: Rewrite Release's doc comment (lines ~216-235) to remove the unproven/false 'load-bearing, prevents double-acquisition' claim and replace it with the verifier-confirmed real behavior: reversing Store(false)/releaseGuard order does not create a correctness/double-acquisition bug (structurally unreachable, since the eviction gate reads the very flag being ordered against), it only delays/breaks eviction progress for that fileID until a later call for the same fileID re-triggers the gate. Cross-reference the new tests that prove both halves.

Add TestFileGuardReleaseOrderingAffectsEvictionProgressNotCorrectness (whitebox, manually replays the reversed order via package-internal access to releaseGuard/fileSplitState, proving no double-acquisition results and that eviction progress is what actually breaks). Add TestFileGuardEvictionGateInProgressClauseIsolated (whitebox, constructs refs==0/inProgress states directly, independent of TryAcquire's refs side effects, isolating the !inProgress clause). Update TestFileGuardRegistryRetainsInProgressEntries's doc comment only if its stated purpose needs correcting relative to the new isolated test (decided: keep it as a black-box/integration-level confirmation, but rephrase 'verifies the eviction gate directly' since it doesn't isolate the clause -- that job now belongs to the new isolated test).

files_not_touched: engine/engine_stress_test.go (stray untracked file, explicitly out of scope, left untouched)
risk: low -- doc-comment correction plus additive whitebox tests in the two files already owned by this subtask; no production logic/behavior change to acquireGuard/releaseGuard/TryAcquire/InProgress. Release's Store(false)-then-releaseGuard ordering is UNCHANGED (still correct/preferred, since it's the only ordering that lets eviction actually progress); only the documented justification changes.
dependencies: None on other in-flight subtasks. Reads (not modifies) engine/btree/latch.go for structural comparison only.
